tools.name: relm_describe_schema
tools.description: Return the live schema: every object, its fields (built-in + custom), and all enum groups with their current values (contact types, activity types, etc). ALWAYS call this before guessing a type, stage, or field - it is the source of truth for what exists.

tools.name: relm_search
tools.description: Cross-entity search over contacts, companies and deals by name/email/title.

tools.name: relm_list
tools.description: List records of an object with keyset pagination. Optional filters vary by object (e.g. contact: email/company_id/type; deal: pipeline/stage/company_id; activity: contact_id/deal_id/type).

tools.name: relm_get
tools.description: Fetch one record by id.

tools.name: relm_create
tools.description: Create a record. `data` is the object's fields (see relm_describe_schema). Custom fields must be registered first (relm_create_field). A contact needs at least one of: a name, an identifier (email/phone/linkedin_url), a company, or a custom field (email is unique per workspace) - don't invent a plac

tools.name: relm_update
tools.description: Patch a record. Pass if_match (the record's current version) for optimistic concurrency; a 412 means re-fetch and retry.

tools.name: relm_delete
tools.description: Soft-delete a record (recoverable via relm_restore). Never hard-destroys data.

tools.name: relm_restore
tools.description: Restore a soft-deleted record.

tools.name: relm_log_activity
tools.description: Log an activity (note/call/email/meeting/task) on a contact and/or deal. Convenience wrapper over create activity.

tools.name: relm_list_pipelines
tools.description: List pipelines with their ordered stages.

tools.name: relm_create_pipeline
tools.description: Create a pipeline. Optionally provide stages [{key,label}] (defaults to lead/qualified/proposal/won/lost) and is_default.

tools.name: relm_manage_stage
tools.description: Add, delete, or reorder stages in a pipeline. action=add requires key (+optional label, +optional ordinal position); action=delete requires key (a stage in use by deals cannot be deleted); action=reorder requires `order` = every existing stage key exactly once, in the new order.

tools.name: relm_create_type
tools.description: Register a new object type in the schema (registry). Idempotent on key; returns the full type set.

tools.name: relm_create_field
tools.description: Register a new custom field on an object (contact/company/deal/activity). data_type: text|number|boolean|date|select|multiselect|currency|reference|email|url. Must be created before it can be written to a record.

tools.name: relm_create_enum_value
tools.description: Add a value to an enum group (e.g. group 'contact.type' value 'partner'). Idempotent; returns the full value set. Use this when relm_create rejects an unknown type with a valid_options list.

tools.name: relm_batch
tools.description: Run up to 100 record writes in ONE call (contact/company/deal/activity) - the fast path for imports/migrations. operations: [{object, method:create|update|delete, data|patch, id?}]. Returns a per-op result array (partial success). Every op still counts against your quota (batch saves round-trips, no

tools.name: relm_automations_capabilities
tools.description: Discover the automation building blocks: valid trigger_events, condition ops, action types, channels/providers, and the shapes of automations/sequences/connections. Call before building a rule or sequence.

tools.name: relm_connect_channel
tools.description: Connect a BYO messaging channel so automations can send. channel='email', provider='resend', api_key=<your Resend key>, from='Team <hi@you.com>' (must be a verified Resend sender). The key is encrypted at rest.

tools.name: relm_list_connections
tools.description: List connected messaging channels (never returns the secret key).

tools.name: relm_create_automation
tools.description: Create a single-step rule: when <trigger_event> [if <conditions>] then <actions>. conditions=[{field,op,value}] (ANDed), actions=[{type,...}]. Example: trigger_event='deal.stage_changed', conditions=[{field:'stage',op:'eq',value:'won'}], actions=[{type:'create_activity',body:'Deal won!'}]. See relm_

tools.name: relm_list_automations
tools.description: List reactive rules (automations).

tools.name: relm_create_template
tools.description: Create a reusable email template. name, html (full HTML, {{field}} placeholders ok), optional subject + text (plaintext alt; auto-derived if omitted). Reference it from a rule/sequence step via {"type":"send_email","template":"<key or tmpl_id>"}.

tools.name: relm_list_templates
tools.description: List reusable email templates.

tools.name: relm_create_sequence
tools.description: Create a multi-step drip sequence. trigger={event,filter:[{field,op,value}]} auto-enrolls the matching entity's contact; exit_when=[{field,op,value}] stops it (re-checked before every send); steps=[{wait_days,subject,body}] (first step wait_days:0 sends now). enroll_existing:true also enrolls everyt

tools.name: relm_list_sequences
tools.description: List drip sequences with their steps and enrollment counts.

tools.name: relm_preview_sequence
tools.description: Dry-run a sequence: how many contacts would enroll now + the send schedule. No side effects.

tools.name: relm_enroll
tools.description: Manually enroll a contact into a sequence (idempotent per contact+sequence). Optional source={object,id} records what drove the enrollment (e.g. a deal).

tools.name: relm_create_webhook
tools.description: Subscribe an https endpoint to CRM events. events=['*'] for all, or a subset of ['contact.created','contact.updated','deal.created','deal.updated','deal.stage_changed']. Returns a `secret` ONCE - the subscriber verifies the `Relm-Signature` header (t=<unix>,v1=<hmac-sha256 of "t.body">). Deliveries

tools.name: relm_list_webhooks
tools.description: List webhook subscriptions in this workspace/mode.

tools.name: relm_delete_webhook
tools.description: Delete a webhook subscription by id.

tools.name: relm_manage_automation
tools.description: Manage an existing automation by id. action=get reads it; action=disable stops it firing (use this to turn off a misbehaving rule); action=enable resumes it; action=delete removes it. To change a rule's trigger/conditions/actions, delete and recreate.

tools.name: relm_manage_sequence
tools.description: Manage an existing drip sequence by id. action=get reads it; action=disable stops further sends (use this to halt a bad sequence); action=enable resumes; action=delete removes it; action=enrollments lists who is enrolled and their state.

tools.name: relm_manage_pipeline
tools.description: Manage an existing pipeline by key or pl_ id. action=get reads it with its stages; action=set_default makes it the workspace default; action=delete removes it (fails if deals still use it - move them first).

tools.name: relm_manage_webhook
tools.description: Inspect or toggle an existing webhook by id. action=get reads it; action=deliveries returns the recent delivery attempts (status, retries, dead-letter) to debug why an endpoint isn't receiving events; action=enable/disable pauses or resumes delivery. Delete is relm_delete_webhook.

tools.name: relm_delete_connection
tools.description: Delete a connected messaging channel by id (e.g. to remove a rotated/wrong Resend key).

tools.name: relm_get_usage
tools.description: Read this workspace's current billing period: requests used, plan, monthly limit, and any metered overage. Call this to see how close you are to the quota before a big import.

tools.name: relm_set_spend_cap
tools.description: Set (or clear) the hard monthly overage spend cap in USD. overage_cap_usd: a number caps paid overage (requests 429 with spend_cap_reached past it), 0 = never any overage, null = unlimited. This is the recovery for a spend_cap_reached error.

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "relm_connect_channel" references sensitive artifacts (api_key). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Connect a BYO messaging channel so automations can send. channel='email', provider='resend', api_key=<your Resend key>, from='Team <hi@you.com>' (must be a veri…

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "relm_list_connections" references sensitive artifacts (secret). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: List connected messaging channels (never returns the secret key).

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "relm_create_webhook" references sensitive artifacts (secret). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Subscribe an https endpoint to CRM events. events=['*'] for all, or a subset of ['contact.created','contact.updated','deal.created','deal.updated','deal.stage_c…

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: relm_search, relm_list, relm_list_templates · Private data: relm_describe_schema, relm_search, relm_list, relm_get, relm_create, relm_update, relm_delete, relm_restore, relm_log_activity, relm_create_field, relm_create_enum_value, relm_batch, relm_list_connections, relm_create_sequence, relm_preview_sequence, relm_enroll, relm_create_webhook · Public sink: relm_search, relm_list, relm_create, relm_log_activity, relm_create_field, relm_connect_channel, relm_create_template, relm_list_templates, relm_create_sequence, relm_preview_sequence, relm_create_webhook, relm_list_webhooks, relm_delete_webhook, relm_manage_webhook · Destructive: relm_delete, relm_manage_stage, relm_batch, relm_preview_sequence, relm_delete_webhook, relm_manage_automation, relm_manage_sequence, relm_manage_pipeline, relm_manage_webhook, relm_delete_connection.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: relm_search, relm_list, relm_list_templates · Private data: relm_describe_schema, relm_search, relm_list, relm_get, relm_create, relm_update, relm_delete, relm_restore, relm_log_activity, relm_create_field, relm_create_enum_value, relm_batch, relm_list_connections, relm_create_sequence, relm_preview_sequence, relm_enroll, relm_create_webhook · Public sink: relm_search, relm_list, relm_create, relm_log_activity, relm_create_field, relm_connect_channel, relm_create_template, relm_list_templates, relm_create_sequence, relm_preview_sequence, relm_create_webhook, relm_list_webhooks, relm_delete_webhook, relm_manage_webhook · Destructive: relm_delete, relm_manage_stage, relm_batch, relm_preview_sequence, relm_delete_webhook, relm_manage_automation, relm_manage_sequence, relm_manage_pipeline, relm_manage_webhook, relm_delete_connection

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
